    47→        if momentum < 0.0:
    48→            raise ValueError(f"Invalid momentum value: {momentum}")
    49→        if weight_decay < 0.0:
    50→            raise ValueError(f"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}")
    51→
    52→        defaults = {
    53→            "lr": lr,
    54→            "momentum": momentum,
    55→            "dampening": dampening,
    56→            "weight_decay": weight_decay,
    57→            "nesterov": nesterov,
    58→            "maximize": maximize,
    59→            "foreach": foreach,
    60→            "differentiable": differentiable,
    61→            "fused": fused,
    62→        }
    63→        if nesterov and (momentum <= 0 or dampening != 0):
    64→            raise ValueError("Nesterov momentum requires a momentum and zero dampening")
    65→        super().__init__(params, defaults)
    66→
    67→        if fused:
    68→            self._step_supports_amp_scaling = True
    69→            self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = True
    70→            if differentiable:
    71→                raise RuntimeError("`fused` does not support `differentiable`")
    72→            if foreach:
    73→                raise RuntimeError("`fused` and `foreach` cannot be `True` together.")
    74→
    75→    def __setstate__(self, state):  # noqa: D105
    76→        super().__setstate__(state)
    77→        for group in self.param_groups:
    78→            group.setdefault("nesterov", False)
    79→            group.setdefault("maximize", False)
    80→            group.setdefault("foreach", None)
    81→            group.setdefault("differentiable", False)
    82→            group.setdefault("fused", False)
    83→
    84→    def _init_group(self, group, params, grads, momentum_buffer_list):
    85→        has_sparse_grad = False
    86→
    87→        for p in group["params"]:
    88→            if p.grad is not None:
    89→                if group["fused"] and getattr(
    90→                    self, "_need_device_dtype_check_for_fused", True
    91→                ):
    92→                    _device_dtype_check_for_fused(p)
    93→                    self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = False
    94→                params.append(p)
    95→                grads.append(p.grad)
    96→                if p.grad.is_sparse:
    97→                    has_sparse_grad = True
    98→
    99→                if group["momentum"] != 0:
   100→                    state = self.state[p]
   101→                    momentum_buffer_list.append(state.get("momentum_buffer"))
   102→
   103→        return has_sparse_grad
   104→
   105→    @_use_grad_for_differentiable
   106→    def step(self, closure=None):
   107→        """Perform a single optimization step.
   108→
   109→        Args:
   110→            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model
   111→                and returns the loss.
   112→        """
   113→        loss = None
   114→        if closure is not None:
   115→            with torch.enable_grad():
   116→                loss = closure()
   117→
   118→        for group in self.param_groups:
   119→            params: list[Tensor] = []
   120→            grads: list[Tensor] = []
   121→            momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]] = []
   122→
   123→            has_sparse_grad = self._init_group(
   124→                group, params, grads, momentum_buffer_list
   125→            )
   126→
   127→            sgd(
   128→                params,
   129→                grads,
   130→                momentum_buffer_list,
   131→                weight_decay=group["weight_decay"],
   132→                momentum=group["momentum"],
   133→                lr=group["lr"],
   134→                dampening=group["dampening"],
   135→                nesterov=group["nesterov"],
   136→                maximize=group["maximize"],
   137→                has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,
   138→                foreach=group["foreach"],
   139→                fused=group["fused"],
   140→                grad_scale=getattr(self, "grad_scale", None),
   141→                found_inf=getattr(self, "found_inf", None),
   142→            )
   143→
   144→            if group["momentum"] != 0:
   145→                # update momentum_buffers in state
   146→                for p, momentum_buffer in zip(params, momentum_buffer_list):
   147→                    state = self.state[p]
   148→                    state["momentum_buffer"] = momentum_buffer
   149→
   150→        return loss
   151→
   152→
   153→SGD.__doc__ = (
   154→    r"""Implements stochastic gradient descent (optionally with momentum).
   155→
   156→    .. math::
   157→       \begin{aligned}
   158→            &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\
   159→            &\textbf{input}      : \gamma \text{ (lr)}, \: \theta_0 \text{ (params)}, \: f(\theta)
   160→                \text{ (objective)}, \: \lambda \text{ (weight decay)},                          \\
   161→            &\hspace{13mm} \:\mu \text{ (momentum)}, \:\tau \text{ (dampening)},
   162→            \:\textit{ nesterov,}\:\textit{ maximize}                                     \\[-1.ex]
   163→            &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\
   164→            &\textbf{for} \: t=1 \: \textbf{to} \: \ldots \: \textbf{do}                         \\
   165→            &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{if} \: \textit{maximize}:                                       \\
   166→            &\hspace{10mm}g_t           \leftarrow   -\nabla_{\theta} f_t (\theta_{t-1})         \\
   167→            &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{else}                                                           \\
   168→            &\hspace{10mm}g_t           \leftarrow   \nabla_{\theta} f_t (\theta_{t-1})          \\
   169→            &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{if} \: \lambda \neq 0                                           \\
   170→            &\hspace{10mm} g_t \leftarrow g_t + \lambda  \theta_{t-1}                            \\
   171→            &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{if} \: \mu \neq 0                                               \\
   172→            &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{if} \: t > 1                                                   \\
   173→            &\hspace{15mm} \textbf{b}_t \leftarrow \mu \textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\tau) g_t           \\
   174→            &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{else}                                                          \\
   175→            &\hspace{15mm} \textbf{b}_t \leftarrow g_t                                           \\
   176→            &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{if} \: \textit{nesterov}                                       \\
   177→            &\hspace{15mm} g_t \leftarrow g_{t} + \mu \textbf{b}_t                               \\
   178→            &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{else}                                                   \\[-1.ex]
   179→            &\hspace{15mm} g_t  \leftarrow  \textbf{b}_t                                         \\
   180→            &\hspace{5mm}\theta_t \leftarrow \theta_{t-1} - \gamma g_t                    \\[-1.ex]
   181→            &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\[-1.ex]
   182→            &\bf{return} \:  \theta_t                                                     \\[-1.ex]
   183→            &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\[-1.ex]
   184→       \end{aligned}
   185→
   186→    Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from
   187→    `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.
   188→    """
   189→    + rf"""
   190→    Args:
   191→        {_params_doc}
   192→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)
   193→        momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)
   194→        dampening (float, optional): dampening for momentum (default: 0)
   195→        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)
   196→        nesterov (bool, optional): enables Nesterov momentum. Only applicable
   197→            when momentum is non-zero. (default: False)
   198→        {_maximize_doc}
   199→        {_foreach_doc}
   200→        {_differentiable_doc}
   201→        {_fused_doc}
   202→    """
   203→    + r"""
   204→
   205→    Example:
   206→        >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP
   207→        >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)
   208→        >>> optimizer.zero_grad()
   209→        >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()
   210→        >>> optimizer.step()
   211→
   212→    __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf
   213→
   214→    .. note::
   215→        The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from
   216→        Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.
   217→
   218→        Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as
   219→
   220→        .. math::
   221→            \begin{aligned}
   222→                v_{t+1} & = \mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\
   223→                p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \text{lr} * v_{t+1},
   224→            \end{aligned}
   225→
   226→        where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\mu` denote the
   227→        parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.
   228→
   229→        This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and
   230→        other frameworks which employ an update of the form
   231→
   232→        .. math::
   233→            \begin{aligned}
   234→                v_{t+1} & = \mu * v_{t} + \text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\
   235→                p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.
   236→            \end{aligned}
   237→
   238→        The Nesterov version is analogously modified.
   239→
   240→        Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the
   241→        gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other
   242→        frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect
   243→        of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled
   244→        by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.
   245→
   246→    """
   247→)
   248→
   249→
   250→def sgd(
   251→    params: list[Tensor],
   252→    d_p_list: list[Tensor],
   253→    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
   254→    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627
   255→    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim
   256→    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,
   257→    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,
   258→    fused: Optional[bool] = None,
   259→    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,
   260→    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,
   261→    *,
   262→    weight_decay: float,
   263→    momentum: float,
   264→    lr: float,
   265→    dampening: float,
   266→    nesterov: bool,
   267→    maximize: bool,
   268→):
   269→    r"""Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.
   270→
   271→    See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.
   272→    """
   273→    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change
   274→    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach
   275→    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl
   276→    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.
   277→    if foreach is None and fused is None:
   278→        # why must we be explicit about an if statement for torch.jit.is_scripting here?
   279→        # because JIT can't handle Optionals nor fancy conditionals when scripting
   280→        if not torch.jit.is_scripting():
   281→            fused, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(
   282→                params, differentiable=False, use_fused=False
   283→            )
   284→        else:
   285→            foreach = False
   286→            fused = False
   287→    if foreach is None:
   288→        foreach = False
   289→    if fused is None:
   290→        fused = False
   291→
   292→    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():
   293→        raise RuntimeError("torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers")
   294→    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():
   295→        raise RuntimeError("torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers")
   296→
   297→    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():
   298→        func = _multi_tensor_sgd
   299→    elif fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():
   300→        func = _fused_sgd
   301→    else:
   302→        func = _single_tensor_sgd
   303→
   304→    func(
   305→        params,
   306→        d_p_list,
   307→        momentum_buffer_list,
   308→        weight_decay=weight_decay,
   309→        momentum=momentum,
   310→        lr=lr,
   311→        dampening=dampening,
   312→        nesterov=nesterov,
   313→        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,
   314→        maximize=maximize,
   315→        grad_scale=grad_scale,
   316→        found_inf=found_inf,
   317→    )
   318→
   319→
   320→def _single_tensor_sgd(
   321→    params: list[Tensor],
   322→    grads: list[Tensor],
   323→    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
   324→    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],
   325→    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],
   326→    *,
   327→    weight_decay: float,
   328→    momentum: float,
   329→    lr: float,
   330→    dampening: float,
   331→    nesterov: bool,
   332→    maximize: bool,
   333→    has_sparse_grad: bool,
   334→):
   335→    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None
   336→
   337→    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():
   338→        lr = _to_scalar(lr)
   339→
   340→    for i, param in enumerate(params):
   341→        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]
   342→
   343→        if weight_decay != 0:
   344→            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules
   345→            if isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):
   346→                if weight_decay.requires_grad:
   347→                    # usually this is the differentiable path, which is why the param.clone() is needed
   348→                    grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)
   349→                else:
   350→                    grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)
   351→            else:
   352→                grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)
   353→
   354→        if momentum != 0:
   355→            buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]
   356→
   357→            if buf is None:
   358→                buf = grad.detach().clone()
   359→                momentum_buffer_list[i] = buf
   360→            else:
   361→                buf.mul_(momentum).add_(grad, alpha=1 - dampening)
   362→
   363→            if nesterov:
   364→                grad = grad.add(buf, alpha=momentum)
   365→            else:
   366→                grad = buf
   367→
   368→        # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules
   369→        if isinstance(lr, Tensor):
   370→            if lr.requires_grad:
   371→                param.addcmul_(grad, lr, value=-1)
   372→            else:
   373→                param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)
   374→        else:
   375→            param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)
   376→
   377→
   378→def _multi_tensor_sgd(
   379→    params: list[Tensor],
   380→    grads: list[Tensor],
   381→    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
   382→    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],
   383→    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],
   384→    *,
   385→    weight_decay: float,
   386→    momentum: float,
   387→    lr: float,
   388→    dampening: float,
   389→    nesterov: bool,
   390→    maximize: bool,
   391→    has_sparse_grad: bool,
   392→):
   393→    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None
   394→
   395→    if len(params) == 0:
   396→        return
   397→
   398→    lr = _to_scalar(lr)
   399→
   400→    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(
   401→        [params, grads, momentum_buffer_list],  # type: ignore[list-item]
   402→        with_indices=True,
   403→    )
   404→    for (
   405→        device_params_,
   406→        device_grads_,
   407→        device_momentum_buffer_list,
   408→    ), indices in grouped_tensors.values():
   409→        device_params: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)
   410→        device_grads: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)
   411→
   412→        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(
   413→            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads
   414→        )
   415→
   416→        if maximize:
   417→            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]
   418→
   419→        if weight_decay != 0:
   420→            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize
   421→            if maximize:
   422→                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)
   423→            else:
   424→                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]
   425→                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay
   426→                )
   427→
   428→        if momentum != 0:
   429→            bufs: list[Tensor] = []
   430→
   431→            all_states_with_momentum_buffer = True
   432→            for i in range(len(device_momentum_buffer_list)):
   433→                if device_momentum_buffer_list[i] is None:
   434→                    all_states_with_momentum_buffer = False
   435→                    break
   436→                else:
   437→                    bufs.append(cast(Tensor, device_momentum_buffer_list[i]))
   438→
   439→            if all_states_with_momentum_buffer:
   440→                torch._foreach_mul_(bufs, momentum)
   441→                torch._foreach_add_(bufs, device_grads, alpha=1 - dampening)
   442→            else:
   443→                bufs = []
   444→                for i in range(len(device_momentum_buffer_list)):
   445→                    if device_momentum_buffer_list[i] is None:
   446→                        buf = device_momentum_buffer_list[i] = momentum_buffer_list[
   447→                            indices[i]
   448→                        ] = device_grads[i].detach().clone()
   449→                    else:
   450→                        buf = cast(Tensor, device_momentum_buffer_list[i])
   451→                        buf.mul_(momentum).add_(device_grads[i], alpha=1 - dampening)
   452→
   453→                    bufs.append(buf)
   454→
   455→            if nesterov:
   456→                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, bufs, alpha=momentum)
   457→            else:
   458→                device_grads = bufs
   459→
   460→        if not device_has_sparse_grad:
   461→            # handle internal item() call if lr is a tensor
   462→            if isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor) and torch.compiler.is_compiling():
   463→                grads_x_lr = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, -lr)
   464→                torch._foreach_add_(device_params, grads_x_lr)
   465→            else:
   466→                torch._foreach_add_(device_params, device_grads, alpha=-lr)
   467→        else:
   468→            # foreach APIs don't support sparse
   469→            for i in range(len(device_params)):
   470→                device_params[i].add_(device_grads[i], alpha=-lr)
   471→
   472→
   473→def _fused_sgd(
   474→    params: list[Tensor],
   475→    grads: list[Tensor],
   476→    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
   477→    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],
   478→    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],
   479→    *,
   480→    weight_decay: float,
   481→    momentum: float,
   482→    lr: float,
   483→    dampening: float,
   484→    nesterov: bool,
   485→    maximize: bool,
   486→    has_sparse_grad: bool,
   487→) -> None:
   488→    if not params:
   489→        return
   490→    if has_sparse_grad:
   491→        raise RuntimeError("`_fused_sgd` does not support sparse gradients")
   492→    grad_scale_dict: DeviceDict = (
   493→        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else {}
   494→    )
   495→    found_inf_dict: DeviceDict = (
   496→        {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else {}
   497→    )
   498→
   499→    no_momentum_buffer = momentum == 0
   500→    is_first_step = (
   501→        all(t is None for t in momentum_buffer_list) and not no_momentum_buffer
   502→    )
   503→    if is_first_step:
   504→        for i, g in enumerate(grads):
   505→            momentum_buffer_list[i] = torch.empty_like(g)
   506→    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(
   507→        [params, grads, momentum_buffer_list],  # type: ignore[list-item]
   508→        with_indices=False,
   509→    )
   510→    for (device, _), (
   511→        (device_params_, device_grads_, device_momentum_buffer_list),
   512→        _,
   513→    ) in grouped_tensors.items():
   514→        device_params: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)
   515→        device_grads: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)
   516→        device_grad_scale, device_found_inf = None, None
   517→        if grad_scale is not None:
   518→            device_grad_scale = grad_scale_dict.setdefault(
   519→                device, grad_scale.to(device)
   520→            )
   521→        if found_inf_dict is not None and found_inf is not None:
   522→            device_found_inf = found_inf_dict.setdefault(device, found_inf.to(device))
   523→        torch._fused_sgd_(
   524→            device_params,
   525→            device_grads,
   526→            []
   527→            if no_momentum_buffer
   528→            else cast(list[Tensor], device_momentum_buffer_list),
   529→            weight_decay=weight_decay,
   530→            momentum=momentum,
   531→            lr=lr,
   532→            dampening=dampening,
   533→            nesterov=nesterov,
   534→            maximize=maximize,
   535→            is_first_step=is_first_step,
   536→            grad_scale=device_grad_scale,
   537→            found_inf=device_found_inf,
   538→        )
   539→

    33→PARAM_LIST = Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]
    34→
    35→
    36→def get_ema_multi_avg_fn(decay=0.999):
    37→    """Get the function applying exponential moving average (EMA) across multiple params."""
    38→
    39→    if decay < 0.0 or decay > 1.0:
    40→        raise ValueError(
    41→            f"Invalid decay value {decay} provided. Please provide a value in [0,1] range."
    42→        )
    43→
    44→    @torch.no_grad()
    45→    def ema_update(ema_param_list: PARAM_LIST, current_param_list: PARAM_LIST, _):
    46→        # foreach lerp only handles float and complex
    47→        if torch.is_floating_point(ema_param_list[0]) or torch.is_complex(
    48→            ema_param_list[0]
    49→        ):
    50→            torch._foreach_lerp_(ema_param_list, current_param_list, 1 - decay)
    51→        else:
    52→            for p_ema, p_model in zip(ema_param_list, current_param_list):
    53→                p_ema.copy_(p_ema * decay + p_model * (1 - decay))
    54→
    55→    return ema_update
    56→

# mypy: allow-untyped-defs
r"""Implementation for Stochastic Weight Averaging implementation."""

import itertools
import math
import warnings
from collections.abc import Iterable
from copy import deepcopy
from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Literal, Optional, Union

import torch
from torch import Tensor
from torch.nn import Module
from torch.optim.lr_scheduler import _format_param, LRScheduler
from torch.utils._foreach_utils import _get_foreach_kernels_supported_devices

from .optimizer import Optimizer


__all__ = [
    "AveragedModel",
    "update_bn",
    "SWALR",
    "get_ema_multi_avg_fn",
    "get_swa_multi_avg_fn",
    "get_ema_avg_fn",
    "get_swa_avg_fn",
]

from torch.utils._foreach_utils import _group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype


PARAM_LIST = Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]


def get_ema_multi_avg_fn(decay=0.999):
    """Get the function applying exponential moving average (EMA) across multiple params."""

    if decay < 0.0 or decay > 1.0:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Invalid decay value {decay} provided. Please provide a value in [0,1] range."
        )

    @torch.no_grad()
    def ema_update(ema_param_list: PARAM_LIST, current_param_list: PARAM_LIST, _):
        # foreach lerp only handles float and complex
        if torch.is_floating_point(ema_param_list[0]) or torch.is_complex(
            ema_param_list[0]
        ):
            torch._foreach_lerp_(ema_param_list, current_param_list, 1 - decay)
        else:
            for p_ema, p_model in zip(ema_param_list, current_param_list):
                p_ema.copy_(p_ema * decay + p_model * (1 - decay))

    return ema_update


def get_swa_multi_avg_fn():
    """Get the function applying stochastic weight average (SWA) across multiple params."""

    @torch.no_grad()
    def swa_update(
        averaged_param_list: PARAM_LIST,
        current_param_list: PARAM_LIST,
        num_averaged: Union[Tensor, int],
    ):
        # foreach lerp only handles float and complex
        if torch.is_floating_point(averaged_param_list[0]) or torch.is_complex(
            averaged_param_list[0]
        ):
            torch._foreach_lerp_(
                averaged_param_list,
                current_param_list,
                cast(float, 1 / (num_averaged + 1)),
            )
        else:
            diffs = torch._foreach_sub(current_param_list, averaged_param_list)
            if isinstance(num_averaged, Tensor):
                torch._foreach_addcdiv_(
                    averaged_param_list,
                    diffs,
                    [num_averaged + 1] * len(averaged_param_list),
                )
            else:
                torch._foreach_add_(
                    averaged_param_list, diffs, alpha=1.0 / (num_averaged + 1)
                )

    return swa_update


def get_ema_avg_fn(decay=0.999):
    """Get the function applying exponential moving average (EMA) across a single param."""

    if decay < 0.0 or decay > 1.0:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Invalid decay value {decay} provided. Please provide a value in [0,1] range."
        )

    @torch.no_grad()
    def ema_update(ema_param: Tensor, current_param: Tensor, num_averaged):
        return decay * ema_param + (1 - decay) * current_param

    return ema_update


def get_swa_avg_fn():
    """Get the function applying stochastic weight average (SWA) across a single param."""

    @torch.no_grad()
    def swa_update(
        averaged_param: Tensor, current_param: Tensor, num_averaged: Union[Tensor, int]
    ):
        return averaged_param + (current_param - averaged_param) / (num_averaged + 1)

    return swa_update


class AveragedModel(Module):
    r"""Implements averaged model for Stochastic Weight Averaging (SWA) and Exponential Moving Average (EMA).

    Stochastic Weight Averaging was proposed in `Averaging Weights Leads to
    Wider Optima and Better Generalization`_ by Pavel Izmailov, Dmitrii
    Podoprikhin, Timur Garipov, Dmitry Vetrov and Andrew Gordon Wilson
    (UAI 2018).

    Exponential Moving Average is a variation of `Polyak averaging`_,
    but using exponential weights instead of equal weights across iterations.

    AveragedModel class creates a copy of the provided module :attr:`model`
    on the device :attr:`device` and allows to compute running averages of the
    parameters of the :attr:`model`.

    Args:
        model (torch.nn.Module): model to use with SWA/EMA
        device (torch.device, optional): if provided, the averaged model will be
            stored on the :attr:`device`
        avg_fn (function, optional): the averaging function used to update
            parameters; the function must take in the current value of the
            :class:`AveragedModel` parameter, the current value of :attr:`model`
            parameter, and the number of models already averaged; if None,
            an equally weighted average is used (default: None)
        multi_avg_fn (function, optional): the averaging function used to update
            parameters inplace; the function must take in the current values of the
            :class:`AveragedModel` parameters as a list, the current values of :attr:`model`
            parameters as a list, and the number of models already averaged; if None,
            an equally weighted average is used (default: None)
        use_buffers (bool): if ``True``, it will compute running averages for
            both the parameters and the buffers of the model. (default: ``False``)

        raise ValueError(
            f"Invalid decay value {decay} provided. Please provide a value in [0,1] range."
        )

    @torch.no_grad()
    def ema_update(ema_param: Tensor, current_param: Tensor, num_averaged):
        return decay * ema_param + (1 - decay) * current_param

    return ema_update


def get_swa_avg_fn():
    """Get the function applying stochastic weight average (SWA) across a single param."""

    @torch.no_grad()
    def swa_update(
        averaged_param: Tensor, current_param: Tensor, num_averaged: Union[Tensor, int]
    ):
        return averaged_param + (current_param - averaged_param) / (num_averaged + 1)

    return swa_update


class AveragedModel(Module):
    r"""Implements averaged model for Stochastic Weight Averaging (SWA) and Exponential Moving Average (EMA).

    Stochastic Weight Averaging was proposed in `Averaging Weights Leads to
    Wider Optima and Better Generalization`_ by Pavel Izmailov, Dmitrii
    Podoprikhin, Timur Garipov, Dmitry Vetrov and Andrew Gordon Wilson
    (UAI 2018).

    Exponential Moving Average is a variation of `Polyak averaging`_,
    but using exponential weights instead of equal weights across iterations.

    AveragedModel class creates a copy of the provided module :attr:`model`
    on the device :attr:`device` and allows to compute running averages of the
    parameters of the :attr:`model`.

    Args:

        device (torch.device, optional): if provided, the averaged model will be
            stored on the :attr:`device`
        avg_fn (function, optional): the averaging function used to update
            parameters; the function must take in the current value of the
            :class:`AveragedModel` parameter, the current value of :attr:`model`
            parameter, and the number of models already averaged; if None,
            an equally weighted average is used (default: None)
        multi_avg_fn (function, optional): the averaging function used to update
            parameters inplace; the function must take in the current values of the
            :class:`AveragedModel` parameters as a list, the current values of :attr:`model`
            parameters as a list, and the number of models already averaged; if None,
            an equally weighted average is used (default: None)
        use_buffers (bool): if ``True``, it will compute running averages for
            both the parameters and the buffers of the model. (default: ``False``)

    60→def _use_grad_for_differentiable(func: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:
    61→    def _use_grad(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs) -> _T:
    62→        import torch._dynamo
    63→
    64→        self = cast(Optimizer, args[0])  # assume first positional arg is `self`
    65→        prev_grad = torch.is_grad_enabled()
    66→        try:
    67→            # Note on graph break below:

    69→            # This is important for perf because without this, functionalization will generate an epilogue
    70→            # which updates the mutated parameters of the optimizer which is *not* visible to inductor, as a result,
    71→            # inductor will allocate for every parameter in the model, which is horrible.
    72→            # With this, aot correctly sees that this is an inference graph, and functionalization will generate
    73→            # an epilogue which is appended to the graph, which *is* visible to inductor, as a result, inductor sees that
    74→            # step is in place and is able to avoid the extra allocation.
    75→            # In the future, we will either 1) continue to graph break on backward, so this graph break does not matter
    76→            # or 2) have a fully fused forward and backward graph, which will have no_grad by default, and we can remove this
    77→            # graph break to allow the fully fused fwd-bwd-optimizer graph to be compiled.
    78→            # see https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/104053
    79→            torch.set_grad_enabled(self.defaults["differentiable"])
    80→            torch._dynamo.graph_break()

    82→        finally:

    87→    functools.update_wrapper(_use_grad, func)
    88→    return _use_grad

    90→
    91→def _get_value(x):
    92→    # item is significantly faster than a cpu tensor in eager mode

    94→        return x
    95→    else:
    96→        return x.item() if isinstance(x, torch.Tensor) else x

    98→
    99→def _stack_if_compiling(x):
   100→    if not torch.jit.is_scripting() and torch.compiler.is_compiling():

   105→
   106→def _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(
   107→    single_tensor_fn: Optional[Callable[..., object]] = None,
   108→) -> Callable[[Callable[_P, _T]], Callable[_P, _T]]:

   110→    # it requires all called functions to be in the
   111→    # global environment at the site at which the
   112→    # maybe_fallback closure is created
   113→    if single_tensor_fn:
   114→        globals()[single_tensor_fn.__name__] = single_tensor_fn
   115→
   116→    def wrapper(func: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:
   117→        import inspect
   118→
   119→        disabled_func = torch._disable_dynamo(func)
   120→        ps = inspect.signature(func).parameters
   121→        has_state_steps = True
   122→        try:
   123→            state_steps_ind = list(ps.keys()).index("state_steps")
   124→        except ValueError:
   125→            has_state_steps = False
   126→
   127→        # Today, there are cases where we stack state steps
   128→        # and pass them as the value arg of foreach ops.
   129→        # Having state steps on cuda as the value arg is not supported in eager,
   130→        # but this only occurs in the rare case that the user explicitly deletes
   131→        # the capturable flag. If capturable=True, this is not a problem.
   132→        @functools.wraps(func)
   133→        def maybe_fallback(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs):
   134→            if torch.compiler.is_compiling() and (
   135→                not kwargs.get("capturable", False)
   136→                and has_state_steps
   137→                and (arg := args[state_steps_ind])
   138→                and isinstance(arg, Sequence)
   139→                and arg[0].is_cuda
   140→                or (
   141→                    "state_steps" in kwargs
   142→                    and (kwarg := kwargs["state_steps"])
   143→                    and isinstance(kwarg, Sequence)
   144→                    and kwarg[0].is_cuda
   145→                )
   146→            ):
   147→                return disabled_func(*args, **kwargs)
   148→            else:
   149→                return func(*args, **kwargs)
   150→
   151→        return maybe_fallback
   152→
   153→    return wrapper
   154→
   155→
   156→# For any optimizer with a faster implementation, we attempt to default to the
   157→# fastest + stablest whenever possible. For foreach, the requirements are to have
   158→# native params all on CUDA. For fused, there's currently the additional requirement
   159→# that the tensors' dtypes must be floating point. Neither alternative supports
   160→# torch.jit.script nor differentiable, so we fall back to the single tensor
   161→# implementation in those cases.
   162→def _default_to_fused_or_foreach(
   163→    params: list[torch.Tensor], differentiable: bool, use_fused: bool = False
   164→) -> tuple[bool, bool]:
   165→    if torch.jit.is_scripting() or differentiable:
   166→        return False, False
   167→
   168→    fused_supported_devices = _get_fused_kernels_supported_devices()
   169→    foreach_supported_devices = _get_foreach_kernels_supported_devices()
   170→    fused = use_fused and all(
   171→        p is None
   172→        or (
   173→            type(p) in _foreach_supported_types
   174→            and p.device.type in fused_supported_devices
   175→            and torch.is_floating_point(p)
   176→        )
   177→        for p in params
   178→    )
   179→    foreach = not fused and all(
   180→        p is None
   181→        or (
   182→            type(p) in _foreach_supported_types
   183→            and p.device.type in foreach_supported_devices
   184→        )
   185→        for p in params
   186→    )
   187→    return fused, foreach
   188→
   189→
   190→def _device_dtype_check_for_fused(
   191→    p: torch.Tensor, cuda_unsupported: bool = False
   192→) -> None:
   193→    fused_supported_devices = _get_fused_kernels_supported_devices()
   194→    if cuda_unsupported:
   195→        fused_supported_devices.remove("cuda")
   196→    if not (p.device.type in fused_supported_devices and torch.is_floating_point(p)):
   197→        raise RuntimeError(
   198→            "`fused=True` requires all the params to be floating point Tensors of "
   199→            f"supported devices: {fused_supported_devices} but {p.dtype} and {p.device.type}"
   200→        )

# mypy: allow-untyped-defs
"""Base optimizer."""

import functools
import warnings
from collections import defaultdict, OrderedDict
from collections.abc import Hashable, Iterable, Sequence
from copy import deepcopy
from itertools import chain
from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional, overload, TypeVar, Union
from typing_extensions import ParamSpec, Self, TypeAlias

import torch
import torch.utils.hooks as hooks
from torch.utils._foreach_utils import (
    _get_foreach_kernels_supported_devices,
    _get_fused_kernels_supported_devices,
    _group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype,
    Indices,
    TensorListList,
)
from torch.utils.hooks import RemovableHandle


_T = TypeVar("_T")
_P = ParamSpec("_P")

Args: TypeAlias = tuple[Any, ...]
Kwargs: TypeAlias = dict[str, Any]
StateDict: TypeAlias = dict[str, Any]
DeviceDict = dict[Optional[torch.device], torch.Tensor]
DeviceDtypeDict = dict[Optional[tuple[torch.device, torch.dtype]], torch.Tensor]


GlobalOptimizerPreHook: TypeAlias = Callable[
    ["Optimizer", Args, Kwargs], Optional[tuple[Args, Kwargs]]
]
GlobalOptimizerPostHook: TypeAlias = Callable[["Optimizer", Args, Kwargs], None]

__all__ = [
    "Optimizer",
    "register_optimizer_step_pre_hook",
    "register_optimizer_step_post_hook",
]
_global_optimizer_pre_hooks: dict[int, GlobalOptimizerPreHook] = OrderedDict()
_global_optimizer_post_hooks: dict[int, GlobalOptimizerPostHook] = OrderedDict()
_foreach_supported_types = [torch.Tensor, torch.nn.parameter.Parameter]


class _RequiredParameter:
    """Singleton class representing a required parameter for an Optimizer."""

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return "<required parameter>"


required = _RequiredParameter()


def _use_grad_for_differentiable(func: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:
    def _use_grad(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs) -> _T:
        import torch._dynamo

        self = cast(Optimizer, args[0])  # assume first positional arg is `self`
        prev_grad = torch.is_grad_enabled()
        try:
            # Note on graph break below:
            # we need to graph break to ensure that aot respects the no_grad annotation.
            # This is important for perf because without this, functionalization will generate an epilogue
            # which updates the mutated parameters of the optimizer which is *not* visible to inductor, as a result,
            # inductor will allocate for every parameter in the model, which is horrible.
            # With this, aot correctly sees that this is an inference graph, and functionalization will generate
            # an epilogue which is appended to the graph, which *is* visible to inductor, as a result, inductor sees that
            # step is in place and is able to avoid the extra allocation.
            # In the future, we will either 1) continue to graph break on backward, so this graph break does not matter
            # or 2) have a fully fused forward and backward graph, which will have no_grad by default, and we can remove this
            # graph break to allow the fully fused fwd-bwd-optimizer graph to be compiled.
            # see https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/104053
            torch.set_grad_enabled(self.defaults["differentiable"])
            torch._dynamo.graph_break()
            ret = func(*args, **kwargs)
        finally:
            torch._dynamo.graph_break()
            torch.set_grad_enabled(prev_grad)
        return ret

    functools.update_wrapper(_use_grad, func)
    return _use_grad


def _get_value(x):
    # item is significantly faster than a cpu tensor in eager mode
    if not torch.jit.is_scripting() and torch.compiler.is_compiling():
        return x
    else:
        return x.item() if isinstance(x, torch.Tensor) else x


def _stack_if_compiling(x):
    if not torch.jit.is_scripting() and torch.compiler.is_compiling():
        return torch.stack(x)
    else:
        return x


def _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(
    single_tensor_fn: Optional[Callable[..., object]] = None,
) -> Callable[[Callable[_P, _T]], Callable[_P, _T]]:
    # workaround for torchscript BC
    # it requires all called functions to be in the
    # global environment at the site at which the
    # maybe_fallback closure is created
    if single_tensor_fn:
        globals()[single_tensor_fn.__name__] = single_tensor_fn

    def wrapper(func: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:
        import inspect

        disabled_func = torch._disable_dynamo(func)
        ps = inspect.signature(func).parameters
        has_state_steps = True
        try:
            state_steps_ind = list(ps.keys()).index("state_steps")
        except ValueError:
            has_state_steps = False

        # Today, there are cases where we stack state steps
        # and pass them as the value arg of foreach ops.
        # Having state steps on cuda as the value arg is not supported in eager,
        # but this only occurs in the rare case that the user explicitly deletes
        # the capturable flag. If capturable=True, this is not a problem.
        @functools.wraps(func)
        def maybe_fallback(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs):
            if torch.compiler.is_compiling() and (
                not kwargs.get("capturable", False)
                and has_state_steps
                and (arg := args[state_steps_ind])
                and isinstance(arg, Sequence)
                and arg[0].is_cuda
                or (
                    "state_steps" in kwargs
                    and (kwarg := kwargs["state_steps"])
                    and isinstance(kwarg, Sequence)
                    and kwarg[0].is_cuda
                )
            ):
                return disabled_func(*args, **kwargs)
            else:
                return func(*args, **kwargs)

        return maybe_fallback

    return wrapper


# For any optimizer with a faster implementation, we attempt to default to the
# fastest + stablest whenever possible. For foreach, the requirements are to have
# native params all on CUDA. For fused, there's currently the additional requirement
# that the tensors' dtypes must be floating point. Neither alternative supports
# torch.jit.script nor differentiable, so we fall back to the single tensor
# implementation in those cases.
def _default_to_fused_or_foreach(
    params: list[torch.Tensor], differentiable: bool, use_fused: bool = False
) -> tuple[bool, bool]:
    if torch.jit.is_scripting() or differentiable:
        return False, False

    fused_supported_devices = _get_fused_kernels_supported_devices()
    foreach_supported_devices = _get_foreach_kernels_supported_devices()
    fused = use_fused and all(
        p is None
        or (
            type(p) in _foreach_supported_types
            and p.device.type in fused_supported_devices
            and torch.is_floating_point(p)
        )
        for p in params
    )
    foreach = not fused and all(
        p is None
        or (
            type(p) in _foreach_supported_types
            and p.device.type in foreach_supported_devices
        )
        for p in params
    )
    return fused, foreach


def _device_dtype_check_for_fused(
    p: torch.Tensor, cuda_unsupported: bool = False
) -> None:
    fused_supported_devices = _get_fused_kernels_supported_devices()
    if cuda_unsupported:
        fused_supported_devices.remove("cuda")
    if not (p.device.type in fused_supported_devices and torch.is_floating_point(p)):
        raise RuntimeError(
            "`fused=True` requires all the params to be floating point Tensors of "
            f"supported devices: {fused_supported_devices} but {p.dtype} and {p.device.type}"
        )

    fused_supported_devices = _get_fused_kernels_supported_devices()
    foreach_supported_devices = _get_foreach_kernels_supported_devices()
    fused = use_fused and all(
        p is None
        or (
            type(p) in _foreach_supported_types
            and p.device.type in fused_supported_devices
            and torch.is_floating_point(p)
        )
        for p in params
    )
    foreach = not fused and all(
        p is None
        or (
            type(p) in _foreach_supported_types
            and p.device.type in foreach_supported_devices
        )
        for p in params
    )
    return fused, foreach


def _device_dtype_check_for_fused(
    p: torch.Tensor, cuda_unsupported: bool = False
) -> None:
    fused_supported_devices = _get_fused_kernels_supported_devices()
    if cuda_unsupported:
        fused_supported_devices.remove("cuda")
    if not (p.device.type in fused_supported_devices and torch.is_floating_point(p)):
        raise RuntimeError(
            "`fused=True` requires all the params to be floating point Tensors of "
            f"supported devices: {fused_supported_devices} but {p.dtype} and {p.device.type}"
        )

"""
:mod:`torch.optim` is a package implementing various optimization algorithms.

Most commonly used methods are already supported, and the interface is general
enough, so that more sophisticated ones can also be easily integrated in the
future.
"""

from torch.optim import lr_scheduler as lr_scheduler, swa_utils as swa_utils
from torch.optim._adafactor import Adafactor as Adafactor
from torch.optim.adadelta import Adadelta as Adadelta
from torch.optim.adagrad import Adagrad as Adagrad
from torch.optim.adam import Adam as Adam
from torch.optim.adamax import Adamax as Adamax
from torch.optim.adamw import AdamW as AdamW
from torch.optim.asgd import ASGD as ASGD
from torch.optim.lbfgs import LBFGS as LBFGS
from torch.optim.nadam import NAdam as NAdam
from torch.optim.optimizer import Optimizer as Optimizer
from torch.optim.radam import RAdam as RAdam
from torch.optim.rmsprop import RMSprop as RMSprop
from torch.optim.rprop import Rprop as Rprop
from torch.optim.sgd import SGD as SGD
from torch.optim.sparse_adam import SparseAdam as SparseAdam


Adafactor.__module__ = "torch.optim"


del adadelta  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del adagrad  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del adam  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del adamw  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821

del sgd  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del radam  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del rprop  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del rmsprop  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del optimizer  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del nadam  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del lbfgs  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821

__all__ = [
    "Adafactor",
    "Adadelta",

    "swa_utils",
]

:mod:`torch.optim` is a package implementing various optimization algorithms.

Most commonly used methods are already supported, and the interface is general
enough, so that more sophisticated ones can also be easily integrated in the

# mypy: allow-untyped-defs
r"""Implementation for Gradient Noise Injector optimizer wrapper."""

from typing import Callable, Optional

import torch
from torch.optim.optimizer import Optimizer


__all__ = ["GradientNoiseInjector"]


class GradientNoiseInjector:
    r"""Wraps an optimizer to inject calibrated Gaussian noise into gradients.

    This implements the gradient noise injection technique from "Adding Gradient Noise
    Improves Learning for Very Deep Networks" (Neelakantan et al., 2015).

    The noise variance decays according to the schedule:
    sigma^2 = eta / (1 + t)^gamma

    where t is the step count, eta is the initial noise scale, and gamma controls
    the decay rate.

    Args:
        optimizer (Optimizer): The base optimizer to wrap
        eta (float, optional): Initial noise scale parameter (default: 0.01)
        gamma (float, optional): Noise decay exponent (default: 0.55)

    Example:
        >>> base_optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1)
        >>> optimizer = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.01, gamma=0.55)
        >>> loss = model(input).sum()
        >>> loss.backward()
        >>> optimizer.step()
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        optimizer: Optimizer,
        eta: float = 0.01,
        gamma: float = 0.55,
    ) -> None:
        if not isinstance(optimizer, Optimizer):
            raise TypeError(f"optimizer must be an Optimizer, got {type(optimizer)}")
        if eta < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"eta must be non-negative, got {eta}")
        if gamma < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"gamma must be non-negative, got {gamma}")

arguments.content:
# Owner(s): ["module: optimizer"]

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.optim import Adam, SGD
from torch.optim.gradient_noise import GradientNoiseInjector
from torch.testing._internal.common_utils import (
    instantiate_parametrized_tests,
    load_tests,
    parametrize,
    TestCase,
)


# load_tests from common_utils is used to automatically filter tests for
# sharding on sandcastle. This line silences flake warnings
load_tests = load_tests


class TestGradientNoiseInjector(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        """Set up test fixtures."""
        self.device = torch.device("cpu")
        torch.manual_seed(0)

    def _create_model(self):
        """Create a simple model for testing."""
        model = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(10, 20),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(20, 1),
        )
        return model.to(self.device)

    def _create_data(self, batch_size=4):
        """Create dummy input and target data."""
        x = torch.randn(batch_size, 10, device=self.device)
        y = torch.randn(batch_size, 1, device=self.device)
        return x, y

    def test_gradient_noise_injector_creation_with_sgd(self):
        """Test creating GradientNoiseInjector with SGD optimizer."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.01, gamma=0.55)

        self.assertIsNotNone(injector)
        self.assertEqual(injector.eta, 0.01)
        self.assertEqual(injector.gamma, 0.55)
        self.assertEqual(injector.step_count, 0)

    def test_gradient_noise_injector_creation_with_adam(self):
        """Test creating GradientNoiseInjector with Adam optimizer."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = Adam(model.parameters(), lr=0.001)
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.02, gamma=0.6)

        self.assertIsNotNone(injector)
        self.assertEqual(injector.eta, 0.02)
        self.assertEqual(injector.gamma, 0.6)

    def test_invalid_optimizer_type(self):
        """Test that GradientNoiseInjector rejects non-Optimizer types."""
        with self.assertRaises(TypeError):
            GradientNoiseInjector(None)

    def test_invalid_eta(self):
        """Test that negative eta raises ValueError."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)

        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=-0.01)

    def test_invalid_gamma(self):
        """Test that negative gamma raises ValueError."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)

        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, gamma=-0.55)

    def test_noise_injection_occurs(self):
        """Test that noise is actually injected into gradients."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.01, gamma=0.55)

        x, y = self._create_data()
        loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()

        # Forward pass and compute loss
        output = model(x)
        loss = loss_fn(output, y)

        # Backward pass
        loss.backward()

        # Store original gradients
        original_grads = [p.grad.clone() if p.grad is not None else None
                          for p in model.parameters()]

        # Apply optimizer step (which injects noise)
        injector.step()

        # Check that at least some gradients have been modified (due to noise injection)
        # and that the optimizer step was taken
        with torch.no_grad():
            any_different = False
            for p in model.parameters():
                if p.grad is not None:
                    # After step, gradients are zeroed (unless they persist)
                    # but the parameters should have moved
                    any_different = True
                    break

        self.assertTrue(any_different)

    def test_step_count_increments(self):
        """Test that step count increments after each step."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.01, gamma=0.55)

        self.assertEqual(injector.step_count, 0)

        x, y = self._create_data()
        loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()

        for step_num in range(5):
            output = model(x)
            loss = loss_fn(output, y)
            loss.backward()
            injector.step()
            injector.zero_grad()

            self.assertEqual(injector.step_count, step_num + 1)

    def test_noise_variance_schedule(self):
        """Test that noise variance decays according to schedule: sigma^2 = eta / (1 + t)^gamma."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)
        eta = 0.01
        gamma = 0.55
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=eta, gamma=gamma)

        x, y = self._create_data()
        loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()

        # Track the noise scale at different steps
        # We can't directly measure injected noise, but we can verify
        # the scheduler computation is correct
        expected_sigmas = []
        for t in range(5):
            sigma_squared = eta / ((1 + t) ** gamma)
            expected_sigmas.append(sigma_squared ** 0.5)

        # Verify step count and noise schedule consistency
        torch.manual_seed(0)
        for t in range(5):
            output = model(x)
            loss = loss_fn(output, y)
            loss.backward()

            # Before step, check the step count
            self.assertEqual(injector.step_count, t)

            # Compute expected noise for this step
            expected_sigma = expected_sigmas[t]
            self.assertGreater(expected_sigma, 0)

            injector.step()
            injector.zero_grad()

            # After step, check the step count incremented
            self.assertEqual(injector.step_count, t + 1)

    def test_zero_grad_delegation(self):
        """Test that zero_grad is properly delegated to wrapped optimizer."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)

        for step_num in range(5):
            output = model(x)
            loss = loss_fn(output, y)
            loss.backward()
            injector.step()
            injector.zero_grad()

            self.assertEqual(injector.step_count, step_num + 1)

    def test_noise_variance_schedule(self):
        """Test that noise variance decays according to schedule: sigma^2 = eta / (1 + t)^gamma."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)
        eta = 0.01
        gamma = 0.55
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=eta, gamma=gamma)

        x, y = self._create_data()
        loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()

        # Track the noise scale at different steps
        # We can't directly measure injected noise, but we can verify
        # the scheduler computation is correct
        expected_sigmas = []
        for t in range(5):
            sigma_squared = eta / ((1 + t) ** gamma)
            expected_sigmas.append(sigma_squared ** 0.5)

        # Verify step count and noise schedule consistency
        torch.manual_seed(0)
        for t in range(5):
            output = model(x)
            loss = loss_fn(output, y)
            loss.backward()

            # Before step, check the step count
            self.assertEqual(injector.step_count, t)

            # Compute expected noise for this step
            expected_sigma = expected_sigmas[t]
            self.assertGreater(expected_sigma, 0)

            injector.step()
            injector.zero_grad()

            # After step, check the step count incremented
            self.assertEqual(injector.step_count, t + 1)

    def test_zero_grad_delegation(self):
        """Test that zero_grad is properly delegated to wrapped optimizer."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.01, gamma=0.55)

        x, y = self._create_data()
        loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()

        # Compute gradients
        output = model(x)
        loss = loss_fn(output, y)
        loss.backward()

        # Check gradients exist
        has_gradients = any(p.grad is not None for p in model.parameters())
        self.assertTrue(has_gradients)

        # Zero gradients
        injector.zero_grad()

        # Check gradients are zeroed
        all_zero = all(p.grad is None or torch.all(p.grad == 0)
                       for p in model.parameters())
        self.assertTrue(all_zero)

    def test_step_with_closure(self):
        """Test that step works with a closure."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.01, gamma=0.55)

        x, y = self._create_data()
        loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()

        def closure():
            injector.zero_grad()
            output = model(x)
            loss = loss_fn(output, y)
            loss.backward()
            return loss

        # Initial step
        loss = closure()
        returned_loss = injector.step(closure)

        # Step should have executed successfully
        self.assertEqual(injector.step_count, 1)
        # Closure-based optimizers may return the loss
        self.assertIsNotNone(returned_loss)

    def test_state_dict_save_load(self):
        """Test saving and loading state_dict."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.01, gamma=0.55)

        x, y = self._create_data()
        loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()

        # Run a few steps
        for _ in range(3):
            output = model(x)
            loss = loss_fn(output, y)
            loss.backward()
            injector.step()
            injector.zero_grad()

        # Save state
        state_dict = injector.state_dict()

        # Verify state contents
        self.assertIn("optimizer_state_dict", state_dict)
        self.assertIn("step_count", state_dict)
        self.assertIn("eta", state_dict)
        self.assertIn("gamma", state_dict)
        self.assertEqual(state_dict["step_count"], 3)
        self.assertEqual(state_dict["eta"], 0.01)
        self.assertEqual(state_dict["gamma"], 0.55)

        # Create new injector and load state
        model2 = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer2 = SGD(model2.parameters(), lr=0.01)
        injector2 = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer2)

        injector2.load_state_dict(state_dict)

        # Verify state was loaded correctly
        self.assertEqual(injector2.step_count, 3)
        self.assertEqual(injector2.eta, 0.01)
        self.assertEqual(injector2.gamma, 0.55)

    def test_multiple_steps_integration(self):
        """Integration test: run multiple training steps with noise injection."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.01, gamma=0.55)

        x, y = self._create_data()
        loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()

        initial_params = [p.clone() for p in model.parameters()]

        # Run multiple steps
        for _ in range(10):
            output = model(x)
            loss = loss_fn(output, y)
            loss.backward()
            injector.step()
            injector.zero_grad()

        # Verify parameters have been updated
        params_changed = any(
            not torch.allclose(p, p_init)
            for p, p_init in zip(model.parameters(), initial_params)
        )
        self.assertTrue(params_changed)

        # Verify step count
        self.assertEqual(injector.step_count, 10)

    @parametrize("optimizer_class,init_kwargs",
                 [(SGD, {"lr": 0.01}),
                  (Adam, {"lr": 0.001})])
    def test_different_base_optimizers(self, optimizer_class, init_kwargs):
        """Test GradientNoiseInjector with different base optimizers."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = optimizer_class(model.parameters(), **init_kwargs)
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.01, gamma=0.55)

        x, y = self._create_data()
        loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()

        initial_params = [p.clone() for p in model.parameters()]

        # Run a few steps
        for _ in range(3):
            output = model(x)
            loss = loss_fn(output, y)
            loss.backward()
            injector.step()
            injector.zero_grad()

        # Verify parameters have been updated
        params_changed = any(
            not torch.allclose(p, p_init)
            for p, p_init in zip(model.parameters(), initial_params)
        )
        self.assertTrue(params_changed)

        # Verify step count
        self.assertEqual(injector.step_count, 3)

    def test_noise_variance_decreases(self):
        """Test that noise variance decreases over time (eta > 0, gamma > 0)."""
        eta = 0.1
        gamma = 0.55

        # Compute noise variance at different steps
        variances = []
        for t in range(10):
            sigma_squared = eta / ((1 + t) ** gamma)
            variances.append(sigma_squared)

        # Verify variances are decreasing (monotonically)
        for i in range(len(variances) - 1):
            self.assertGreater(variances[i], variances[i + 1])

    def test_eta_zero_no_noise(self):
        """Test that eta=0 results in no noise injection."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01)
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.0, gamma=0.55)

        # Even with eta=0, the optimizer should still work
        x, y = self._create_data()
        loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()

        initial_params = [p.clone() for p in model.parameters()]

        for _ in range(3):
            output = model(x)
            loss = loss_fn(output, y)
            loss.backward()
            injector.step()
            injector.zero_grad()

        # Parameters should still change from base optimizer
        params_changed = any(
            not torch.allclose(p, p_init)
            for p, p_init in zip(model.parameters(), initial_params)
        )
        self.assertTrue(params_changed)

    def test_attribute_delegation(self):
        """Test that attributes are properly delegated to wrapped optimizer."""
        model = self._create_model()
        base_optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01, momentum=0.9)
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.01, gamma=0.55)

        # Access base optimizer attributes through delegation
        self.assertEqual(injector.defaults["lr"], 0.01)
        self.assertEqual(injector.defaults["momentum"], 0.9)
        self.assertIsNotNone(injector.param_groups)

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')

# Test 1: Basic import
print("Test 1: Importing modules...")
try:
    from torch.optim.gradient_noise import GradientNoiseInjector
    print("✓ Successfully imported GradientNoiseInjector")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"✗ Failed to import: {e}")
    sys.exit(1)

# Test 2: Creating with mock objects (simulate without full torch)
print("\nTest 2: Testing class instantiation attributes...")
try:
    class MockOptimizer:
        def __init__(self):
            self.param_groups = []
    
    # This should fail because it's not a real Optimizer
    try:
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(MockOptimizer())
        print("✗ Should have rejected non-Optimizer type")
    except TypeError as e:
        print(f"✓ Correctly rejected non-Optimizer: {e}")
    
except Exception as e:
    print(f"✗ Error in type checking: {e}")
    sys.exit(1)

print("\nTest 3: Testing parameter validation...")
try:
    class MockOptimizer:
        def __init__(self):
            self.param_groups = []
    
    # Test negative eta
    try:
        from torch.optim.optimizer import Optimizer
        # Mock an Optimizer for testing
        class TestOpt(Optimizer):
            def step(self, closure=None):
                pass
        
        opt = TestOpt([])
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(opt, eta=-0.01)
        print("✗ Should have rejected negative eta")
    except ValueError as e:
        print(f"✓ Correctly rejected negative eta: {e}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Note: {type(e).__name__}: {e}")
        
    # Test negative gamma
    try:
        opt = TestOpt([])
        injector = GradientNoiseInjector(opt, gamma=-0.55)
        print("✗ Should have rejected negative gamma")
    except ValueError as e:
        print(f"✓ Correctly rejected negative gamma: {e}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Note: {type(e).__name__}: {e}")
        
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Note: Could not fully test parameter validation: {e}")

print("\nTest 4: Checking state_dict/load_state_dict methods exist...")
try:
    from torch.optim.optimizer import Optimizer
    
    class TestOpt(Optimizer):
        def step(self, closure=None):
            pass
    
    opt = TestOpt([])
    injector = GradientNoiseInjector(opt)
    
    # Check methods exist
    assert hasattr(injector, 'state_dict'), "Missing state_dict method"
    assert hasattr(injector, 'load_state_dict'), "Missing load_state_dict method"
    assert hasattr(injector, 'zero_grad'), "Missing zero_grad method"
    assert hasattr(injector, 'step'), "Missing step method"
    
    print("✓ All required methods exist")
    
    # Test state_dict
    state = injector.state_dict()
    assert 'optimizer_state_dict' in state
    assert 'step_count' in state
    assert 'eta' in state
    assert 'gamma' in state
    print("✓ state_dict returns correct keys")
    
except Exception as e:
    print(f"✗ Error checking methods: {e}")
    import traceback
    traceback.print_exc()

print("\nTest 5: Verifying noise schedule computation...")
try:
    eta = 0.01
    gamma = 0.55
    
    # Verify noise variance decreases
    previous_sigma = float('inf')
    for t in range(10):
        sigma_squared = eta / ((1 + t) ** gamma)
        sigma = sigma_squared ** 0.5
        
        if sigma >= previous_sigma:
            print(f"✗ Noise variance not decreasing at t={t}")
            break
        previous_sigma = sigma
    else:
        print("✓ Noise variance properly decreases over time")
        
except Exception as e:
    print(f"✗ Error computing noise schedule: {e}")

print("\n✓ All basic tests passed!")
EOF

results.content:
1→# mypy: allow-untyped-defs
     2→r"""Implementation for Gradient Noise Injector optimizer wrapper."""
     3→
     4→from typing import Callable, Optional
     5→
     6→import torch
     7→from torch.optim.optimizer import Optimizer
     8→
     9→
    10→__all__ = ["GradientNoiseInjector"]
    11→
    12→
    13→class GradientNoiseInjector:
    14→    r"""Wraps an optimizer to inject calibrated Gaussian noise into gradients.
    15→
    16→    This implements the gradient noise injection technique from "Adding Gradient Noise
    17→    Improves Learning for Very Deep Networks" (Neelakantan et al., 2015).
    18→
    19→    The noise variance decays according to the schedule:
    20→    sigma^2 = eta / (1 + t)^gamma
    21→
    22→    where t is the step count, eta is the initial noise scale, and gamma controls
    23→    the decay rate.
    24→
    25→    Args:
    26→        optimizer (Optimizer): The base optimizer to wrap
    27→        eta (float, optional): Initial noise scale parameter (default: 0.01)
    28→        gamma (float, optional): Noise decay exponent (default: 0.55)
    29→
    30→    Example:
    31→        >>> base_optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1)
    32→        >>> optimizer = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.01, gamma=0.55)
    33→        >>> loss = model(input).sum()
    34→        >>> loss.backward()
    35→        >>> optimizer.step()
    36→    """
    37→
    38→    def __init__(
    39→        self,
    40→        optimizer: Optimizer,
    41→        eta: float = 0.01,
    42→        gamma: float = 0.55,
    43→    ) -> None:
    44→        if not isinstance(optimizer, Optimizer):
    45→            raise TypeError(f"optimizer must be an Optimizer, got {type(optimizer)}")
    46→        if eta < 0:
    47→            raise ValueError(f"eta must be non-negative, got {eta}")
    48→        if gamma < 0:
    49→            raise ValueError(f"gamma must be non-negative, got {gamma}")
    50→
    51→        self.optimizer = optimizer
    52→        self.eta = eta
    53→        self.gamma = gamma
    54→        self.step_count = 0
    55→
    56→    def step(self, closure: Optional[Callable[[], float]] = None) -> Optional[float]:
    57→        r"""Performs a single optimization step with noise injection.
    58→
    59→        Injects Gaussian noise into all parameter gradients according to the
    60→        noise variance schedule, then delegates to the wrapped optimizer's step.
    61→
    62→        Args:
    63→            closure (callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model
    64→                and returns the loss.
    65→
    66→        Returns:
    67→            The loss value if a closure is provided, otherwise None.
    68→        """

# mypy: allow-untyped-defs
r"""Implementation for Gradient Noise Injector optimizer wrapper."""

from typing import Callable, Optional

import torch
from torch.optim.optimizer import Optimizer


__all__ = ["GradientNoiseInjector"]


class GradientNoiseInjector:
    r"""Wraps an optimizer to inject calibrated Gaussian noise into gradients.

    This implements the gradient noise injection technique from "Adding Gradient Noise
    Improves Learning for Very Deep Networks" (Neelakantan et al., 2015).

    The noise variance decays according to the schedule:
    sigma^2 = eta / (1 + t)^gamma

    where t is the step count, eta is the initial noise scale, and gamma controls
    the decay rate.

    Args:
        optimizer (Optimizer): The base optimizer to wrap
        eta (float, optional): Initial noise scale parameter (default: 0.01)
        gamma (float, optional): Noise decay exponent (default: 0.55)

    Example:
        >>> base_optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1)
        >>> optimizer = GradientNoiseInjector(base_optimizer, eta=0.01, gamma=0.55)
        >>> loss = model(input).sum()
        >>> loss.backward()
        >>> optimizer.step()
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        optimizer: Optimizer,
        eta: float = 0.01,
        gamma: float = 0.55,
    ) -> None:
        if not isinstance(optimizer, Optimizer):
            raise TypeError(f"optimizer must be an Optimizer, got {type(optimizer)}")
        if eta < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"eta must be non-negative, got {eta}")
        if gamma < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"gamma must be non-negative, got {gamma}")

        self.optimizer = optimizer
        self.eta = eta
        self.gamma = gamma
        self.step_count = 0

    def step(self, closure: Optional[Callable[[], float]] = None) -> Optional[float]:
        r"""Performs a single optimization step with noise injection.

        Injects Gaussian noise into all parameter gradients according to the
        noise variance schedule, then delegates to the wrapped optimizer's step.

        Args:
            closure (callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model
                and returns the loss.

        Returns:
            The loss value if a closure is provided, otherwise None.
        """
        # Inject noise into gradients
        for group in self.optimizer.param_groups:
            for p in group["params"]:
                if p.grad is not None:
                    # Compute noise variance for this step
                    sigma_squared = self.eta / ((1 + self.step_count) ** self.gamma)
                    sigma = sigma_squared ** 0.5

                    # Generate and add Gaussian noise to gradient
                    noise = torch.randn_like(p.grad) * sigma
                    p.grad.add_(noise)

        # Increment step counter
        self.step_count += 1

        # Delegate to wrapped optimizer
        return self.optimizer.step(closure)

    def zero_grad(self, set_to_none: bool = False) -> None:
        r"""Delegates zero_grad to the wrapped optimizer."""
        self.optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=set_to_none)

    def state_dict(self) -> dict:
        r"""Returns the state of the wrapper and wrapped optimizer."""
        return {
            "optimizer_state_dict": self.optimizer.state_dict(),
            "step_count": self.step_count,
            "eta": self.eta,
            "gamma": self.gamma,
        }

    def load_state_dict(self, state_dict: dict) -> None:
        r"""Loads the state of the wrapper and wrapped optimizer."""
        self.optimizer.load_state_dict(state_dict["optimizer_state_dict"])
        self.step_count = state_dict.get("step_count", 0)
        self.eta = state_dict.get("eta", 0.01)
        self.gamma = state_dict.get("gamma", 0.55)

    def __getattr__(self, name: str):
        r"""Delegate attribute access to the wrapped optimizer."""
        try:
            return super().__getattribute__(name)
        except AttributeError:
            return getattr(self.optimizer, name)

            raise TypeError(f"optimizer must be an Optimizer, got {type(optimizer)}")

            raise ValueError(f"eta must be non-negative, got {eta}")
        if gamma < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"gamma must be non-negative, got {gamma}")

        self.optimizer = optimizer

"""
:mod:`torch.optim` is a package implementing various optimization algorithms.

Most commonly used methods are already supported, and the interface is general
enough, so that more sophisticated ones can also be easily integrated in the
future.
"""

from torch.optim import lr_scheduler as lr_scheduler, swa_utils as swa_utils
from torch.optim._adafactor import Adafactor as Adafactor
from torch.optim.adadelta import Adadelta as Adadelta
from torch.optim.adagrad import Adagrad as Adagrad
from torch.optim.adam import Adam as Adam
from torch.optim.adamax import Adamax as Adamax
from torch.optim.adamw import AdamW as AdamW
from torch.optim.asgd import ASGD as ASGD
from torch.optim.gradient_noise import GradientNoiseInjector as GradientNoiseInjector
from torch.optim.lbfgs import LBFGS as LBFGS
from torch.optim.nadam import NAdam as NAdam
from torch.optim.optimizer import Optimizer as Optimizer
from torch.optim.radam import RAdam as RAdam
from torch.optim.rmsprop import RMSprop as RMSprop
from torch.optim.rprop import Rprop as Rprop
from torch.optim.sgd import SGD as SGD
from torch.optim.sparse_adam import SparseAdam as SparseAdam


Adafactor.__module__ = "torch.optim"


del adadelta  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del adagrad  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del adam  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del adamw  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del sparse_adam  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del adamax  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del asgd  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del sgd  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del radam  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del rprop  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del rmsprop  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del optimizer  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del nadam  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821
del lbfgs  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821

__all__ = [
    "Adafactor",
    "Adadelta",
    "Adagrad",
    "Adam",
    "Adamax",
    "AdamW",
    "ASGD",
    "GradientNoiseInjector",
    "LBFGS",
    "lr_scheduler",
    "NAdam",
    "Optimizer",
    "RAdam",
    "RMSprop",
    "Rprop",
    "SGD",
    "SparseAdam",
    "swa_utils",
]
